content.problemStatement:
Almir had a small sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. He decided to make KKK copies of this sequence and concatenate them, forming a sequence X1,X2,‚Ä¶,XNKX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_{NK}X1‚Äã,X2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,XNK‚Äã; for each valid iii and jjj (0‚â§j<K0 \le j \lt K0‚â§j<K), Xj‚ãÖN+i=AiX_{j \cdot N + i} = A_iXj‚ãÖN+i‚Äã=Ai‚Äã.

For example, if A=(1,2,3)A = (1, 2, 3)A=(1,2,3) and K=4K = 4K=4, the final sequence is X=(1,2,3,1,2,3,1,2,3,1,2,3)X = (1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3)X=(1,2,3,1,2,3,1,2,3,1,2,3).

A pair (i,j)(i, j)(i,j), where 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚â§i<j‚â§N, is an inversion if Xi>XjX_i \gt X_jXi‚Äã>Xj‚Äã. Find the number of inversions in the final sequence XXX.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the number of inversions in the sequence XXX.
